title: Scalar Functions
content:
### Scalar Functions

Scalar functions operate on individual values and return a single value per row. They can be used in SELECT, WHERE, and other clauses.

---

### String Scalar Functions

| Function | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| UPPER() | Converts to uppercase | UPPER('hello') => 'HELLO' |
| LOWER() | Converts to lowercase | LOWER('HELLO') => 'hello' |
| LENGTH() | Returns string length | LENGTH('hello') => 5 |
| TRIM() | Removes leading/trailing spaces | TRIM(' hello ') => 'hello' |
| SUBSTRING() | Extracts part of a string | SUBSTRING('hello', 1, 2) => 'he' |
| REPLACE() | Replaces occurrences | REPLACE('abc', 'b', 'x') => 'axc' |

### Numeric Scalar Functions

| Function | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| ROUND() | Rounds to specified decimals | ROUND(3.14159, 2) => 3.14 |
| ABS() | Absolute value | ABS(-5) => 5 |
| CEIL() | Rounds up to nearest integer | CEIL(3.1) => 4 |
| FLOOR() | Rounds down to nearest integer | FLOOR(3.9) => 3 |
| MOD() | Remainder of division | MOD(10, 3) => 1 |

### Date Scalar Functions

| Function | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| NOW() | Current date and time | NOW() |
| CURDATE() | Current date | CURDATE() |
| YEAR() | Extracts the year | YEAR('2024-01-15') => 2024 |
| MONTH() | Extracts the month | MONTH('2024-01-15') => 1 |
| DAY() | Extracts the day | DAY('2024-01-15') => 15 |

> **Key Point:** Scalar functions return one result per row, unlike aggregate functions which return one result per group.
codeExample:
SELECT name,
       UPPER(name) AS upper_name,
       LENGTH(name) AS name_length,
       ROUND(salary * 1.1, 0) AS increased_salary
FROM employees;

quiz.question: Scalar functions return:
options: One result per group of rows
One result for the entire query
